title: China-to-Tunisia BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Basis and Tunisia Site Electrical Settings
rows.target_requirement: China and Tunisia normally use a 50 Hz design basis, reducing first-pass screening risk for PCS, transformers, auxiliary supplies, motors, relays, and metering. This does not by itself satisfy STEG interconnection acceptance. The Tunisia point of connection must confirm voltage class, frequency and voltage ride-through, reactive-power control, harmonics, protection settings, grounding, metering, SCADA, dispatch, and commissioning tests.
target_standards: STEG grid-connection and commissioning requirements
Project-specific frequency and voltage operating windows
PCS, transformer, protection, metering, and SCADA settings for the Tunisia point of connection
rows.china_equivalent: China factory PCS settings and 50 Hz type-test evidence are useful starting data. They still need Tunisia site validation for voltage class, transformer taps, relay coordination, harmonics, grounding, SCADA mapping, and commissioning records.
rows.gap: Do not rely only on the 50 Hz match. Provide Tunisia-specific protection settings, power-quality study, short-circuit study, transformer configuration, SCADA mapping, commissioning plan, and site acceptance records.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where STEG interconnection, grid-code, EPC, owner, insurer, lender, or commissioning approval applies; detailed settings are project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected projects through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The 50 Hz match reduces equipment-screening friction but is not Tunisia interconnection approval; site settings and commissioning remain separate requirements.

source.publisher: Société Tunisienne de l'Electricité et du Gaz (STEG)

rows.requirement: ANME Renewable-Energy Documentation and Import Classification
rows.target_requirement: ANME publishes renewable-energy, self-generation, installer, accepted-equipment, and regulatory resources for Tunisia energy projects. For BESS paired with PV, self-generation, or utility projects, exporters should confirm whether ANME program documents, accepted-equipment lists, tender documents, incentives, or energy-ministry approvals impose equipment, installer, technical-file, or local-language requirements. Separately, each BESS component should be classified for customs and import treatment by HS code.
target_standards: ANME renewable-energy and self-generation resources
ANME eligible installer and accepted-equipment lists where applicable
Tunisia customs and import classification by HS code
rows.china_equivalent: China export files commonly include commercial invoices, packing lists, HS classifications, certificates of origin, China test reports, and factory quality documents. These should be reconciled with Tunisia customs classification and any ANME or project-specific technical-file expectations.
china_standards: GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 technical reports where relevant
rows.gap: Before shipment, map cells, modules, racks, PCS, containers, HVAC, fire systems, transformers, switchgear, and controls to HS codes and project documents. Confirm whether the Tunisia route requires French or Arabic labels, manuals, warranty terms, local installer evidence, or accepted-equipment listing.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Tunisia customs, ANME program rules, energy-ministry approval, tender, incentive, owner, insurer, lender, or contract calls up the import or project-document route; otherwise project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for China export documentation and customs treatment; China technical reports remain support unless required by Tunisia project documents

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat ANME and import documentation as a project gate, especially for PV-paired or self-generation BESS. Align HS codes, local-language documents, and accepted-equipment expectations before shipment.

source.publisher: Agence Nationale pour la Maîtrise de l'Energie (ANME)

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Crosswalk to Tunisia Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Tunisia reviewers may accept China GB evidence as technical support only if the tender, owner, insurer, lender, authority, or conformity route allows it. For many international BESS projects, IEC-numbered reports, French or English technical files, and project-specific STEG / fire / import evidence are more directly reviewable than China-only GB reports.
target_standards: INNORPI / project standards acceptance route
IEC 62933 series
STEG and project-specific technical requirements
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 is a China mandatory standard for safety requirements of electrochemical energy storage stations in its applicable scope, with an implementation date of 2025-08-01. GB/T 36276-2023 is a recommended China standard for lithium-ion batteries for electrical energy storage. They are central China-side references but are not Tunisia standards unless specifically accepted or incorporated.
rows.gap: Create a bilingual crosswalk that lists each China GB report, the exact product configuration tested, deviations from the Tunisia project design, equivalent IEC clauses, unresolved tests, and whether the buyer or authority accepts China GB evidence as support or requires IEC retesting.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary support unless the Tunisia tender, authority, project owner, insurer, lender, or contract expressly accepts or requires China GB evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China from its 2025-08-01 implementation date; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless incorporated by regulation or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB evidence is useful but not self-executing in Tunisia. Confirm whether the Tunisia route accepts it or requires IEC reports and local project evidence.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform (China)

rows.requirement: Fire-Safety Review for Stationary Lithium BESS Installations
rows.target_requirement: The official public material reviewed did not identify one Tunisia BESS-only fire certificate. Fire safety should therefore be handled as an installation and project-permit issue involving the local authority having jurisdiction, site owner, insurer, EPC, and emergency responders. A BESS fire package should cover layout, separation, container rating, ventilation, gas detection where relevant, thermal-runaway mitigation, emergency shutdown, firefighting access, water or agent strategy, signage, and emergency procedures in the required local language.
target_standards: Tunisia local fire-authority and building-permit review where applicable
Project-specific fire-protection design approval
IEC 62933 safety evidence where called up by tender, owner, insurer, or authority
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS files may include GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, container fire-system specifications, and Chinese project fire-review evidence. These can support Tunisia due diligence but do not replace a Tunisia site fire review.
rows.gap: Provide Tunisia site drawings, hazard analysis, battery chemistry and thermal-runaway data, fire detection and suppression design, HVAC and ventilation evidence, emergency shutdown logic, emergency response plan, and commissioning records for review by the authority, owner, EPC, and insurer.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where local fire, building, site-permit, owner, insurer, lender, EPC, or tender review applies; no single public Tunisia BESS-only fire certificate was identified in the reviewed official sources
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval, local fire review, and contract requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A battery or IEC report alone is not a Tunisia fire approval. Build a site-specific fire-safety package for the local authority, owner, EPC, and insurer.

source.publisher: Office National de la Protection Civile (Tunisia)

rows.requirement: STEG / ANME Storage, Renewable-Energy, and Grid-Connection Review
rows.target_requirement: A Tunisia grid-connected BESS should be treated as a project-specific utility and energy-regulatory review. STEG is the electricity utility and grid interface, while ANME publishes renewable-energy, self-generation, and energy-transition resources and lists for PV-related programs. Depending on the project model, BESS may need STEG connection studies, protection and metering approval, dispatch or operating conditions, ANME / energy-ministry renewable-energy documentation, and contract-specific storage controls.
target_standards: STEG grid-connection and operating requirements
ANME renewable-energy and self-generation documentation
Project-specific power-purchase, self-generation, tender, or concession documents
rows.china_equivalent: China-side grid files commonly include GB/T 36558-2023, PCS factory settings, protection studies, and grid-company commissioning records. They are useful evidence but do not replace STEG connection approval or Tunisia project authorization.
rows.gap: Prepare a Tunisia-specific grid package covering point of connection, voltage class, short-circuit duty, protection coordination, anti-islanding, active and reactive power control, ramp-rate limits, metering, SCADA / telemetry, dispatch instructions, commissioning tests, and storage operating modes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where STEG connection, ANME / energy-ministry project route, tender, PPA, self-generation approval, owner, insurer, or lender requires approval; details are project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected BESS through grid-company technical review and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China grid evidence is not STEG acceptance. Treat Tunisia BESS interconnection as a project-specific STEG / ANME and contract review.

source.publisher: Société Tunisienne de l'Electricité et du Gaz (STEG)

rows.requirement: INNORPI Standards Route and IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 Evidence
rows.target_requirement: INNORPI is Tunisia's national standards and conformity body. For stationary lithium BESS, the official public material reviewed did not identify a single Tunisia-only compulsory BESS product certificate. IEC 62619:2022 battery safety evidence and relevant IEC 62933 electrical-energy-storage-system evidence should be treated as likely technical evidence for standards, tender, owner, insurer, lender, customs, or project review unless INNORPI, a legal instrument, or a contract expressly makes a specific Tunisian or IEC edition mandatory.
target_standards: INNORPI national standardization and conformity assessment services
IEC 62933 series, including safety and system requirements where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS files commonly include GB 44240-2024 for electrochemical energy-storage station safety and GB/T 36276-2023 for lithium-ion batteries for electrical energy storage. These can support a technical comparison but should not be assumed to replace IEC-numbered reports or Tunisia acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare an English or French clause matrix mapping GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 to IEC 62619:2022 and the relevant IEC 62933 parts. Clearly separate formal IEC test reports from China-only GB reports, and confirm with INNORPI, customs, or the project owner whether IEC evidence is mandatory for the exact HS codes and project route.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific or mandatory only where INNORPI, customs, tender, insurer, lender, owner, utility, permit, or contract calls up the standard or conformity route
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory where applicable in China; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless incorporated by regulation or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not treat China GB reports as automatic Tunisia acceptance. Confirm the INNORPI / project route and provide IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 evidence where it is called up.

source.publisher: Institut National de la Normalisation et de la Propriété Industrielle (INNORPI)

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells, modules, and battery packs shipped to Tunisia normally need dangerous-goods transport evidence under the UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, subsection 38.3, plus the applicable modal rules for sea, air, road, or multimodal transport. UN 38.3 is transport evidence, not an installation approval or Tunisia product certification.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, subsection 38.3
UN Model Regulations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods
IMDG Code / ICAO Technical Instructions / IATA DGR where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China exporters commonly hold UN 38.3 test summaries, MSDS / SDS, dangerous-goods classification reports, and packaging certificates. These documents are directly useful for Tunisia shipments if they identify the same cells, modules, packs, configuration, state of charge, and packaging used in the consignment.
china_standards: China export packaging certificate where applicable
rows.gap: Check that UN 38.3 reports cover the exact battery design and that the test summary, SDS, emergency contact, labels, marks, overpack information, and shipping declaration match the selected transport mode and Tunisia consignee documentation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for lithium battery dangerous-goods transport where UN 38.3 and modal transport rules apply; not a substitute for Tunisia installation, grid, or fire approval
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for covered lithium battery export transport under dangerous-goods transport rules

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 is a shipment gate for lithium batteries, but it does not close Tunisia grid, fire, or project-approval gaps.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
